sample_size.source_quote: A total of 4,910 patients were included in the CCCST. Of those, 2,086 (42.5%) had sepsis, including 1,134 (54.4%) with septic shock

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: The ICU mortality rates were 27.2% in patients with sepsis and 9.5% in those without sepsis

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, APACHE II score, comorbid cardiovascular diseases and tumors, infection with Acinetobacter/Pseudomonas/Staphylococcus, use of RRT, antibiotic timing, no comorbid conditions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Patients with septic shock had a nearly 3-fold increased risk of mortality than those without shock (OR 2.92, 95% CI 2.04–4.17, Table 7)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Antibiotics within 1 h of sepsis onset
mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: ICU mortality in patients with sepsis
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, APACHE II score, comorbid cardiovascular diseases and tumors, infection with Acinetobacter/Pseudomonas/Staphylococcus, use of RRT, septic shock, no comorbid conditions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the use of antibiotics within 1 h of sepsis onset were associated with relatively lower ICU mortality in patients with sepsis ... administering antibiotics within 1 h (OR 0.65, 95% CI, 0.46–0.92) of the onset of septic shock decreased the odds ratio for mortality in patients with septic shock

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Resuscitation goal of 30 ml/kg fluid bolus within first 3 h
mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: ICU mortality in patients with septic shock
mortality_predictors.timing: First 3 hours after onset of septic shock
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, APACHE II score, comorbid cardiovascular diseases and tumors, infection with Acinetobacter/Pseudomonas/Staphylococcus, use of RRT, antibiotic timing, no comorbid conditions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Achieving the resuscitation goal of 30 ml/kg during the ﬁrst 3 h (OR 0.43, 95% CI, 0.30–0.63) and administering antibiotics within 1 h (OR 0.65, 95% CI, 0.46–0.92) of the onset of septic shock decreased the odds ratio for mortality in patients with septic shock
According to the univariate analysis, lung infection was associated with high ICU mortality, and wound/soft tissue infection was associated with low mortality.

mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: ICU mortality in patients with sepsis
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, APACHE II score, comorbid cardiovascular diseases and tumors, use of RRT, septic shock, antibiotic timing, no comorbid conditions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: infection with Acinetobacter, Pseudomonas, and Staphylococcus; and the use of RRT were independent risk factors for ICU mortality in patients with sepsis
